instruction: Using Terminal, count how many files with extension .{EXT} are in ~/scan and write the count to ~/scan/count.txt.
source: cove-original (design 047 §5 Tier-A). Multi-step shell task; baseline seeds a known number of files so the answer is deterministic and the no-op (empty count) scores 0.

args: rm -rf "$HOME/scan"; mkdir -p "$HOME/scan"; for i in 1 2 3; do : > "$HOME/scan/f$i.{EXT}"; done; : > "$HOME/scan/other.dat"
